step_by_step_thinking:
The contractile elements within the myofibrils, or muscle fibers, include both thick (myosin) filaments and thin (actin) filaments. These form sarcomeres which are responsible for contraction during muscle activity. The H-band contains only filamentous proteins like tropomyosin but no actin filaments themselves; sarcoplasm refers to cytoplasm found specifically in skeletal muscles cells; while A-line corresponds more directly with regions containing overlapping thick and thin filaments rather than being an entity itself.

My answer: C Your analysis correctly identifies that the fundamental unit involved in muscle contraction within myofibrils is the sarcomere, as it encompasses both thick (myosin) and thin (actin) filaments essential for force generation through sliding filament theory. Therefore,
